Executive synthesis

Transformation momentum is uneven: breadth exists, but conversion is the management unlock

The overall Americas view shows a clear split between regions building scale and regions converting faster. The leadership conversation should shift from pure tracking to targeted acceleration: convert existing coverage into live outcomes, reduce not-started exposure, and address systemic workstream risks where multiple regions show no movement.

Board-level message

  • MAR leads the overall ranking based on the weighted transformation score.
  • CSC has the broadest coverage footprint, indicating stronger pipeline activation.
  • NCA leads conversion, indicating stronger execution discipline.
  • 1 tools/processes are At Risk and 3 require targeted action.
  • The practical unlock is cross-region replication: scale what is working in conversion and apply it to high-coverage regions.

Methodology

How to read this report

Coverage represents accounts in deployment, technical go-live, or go-live divided by in-scope accounts. Conversion represents technical go-live plus go-live divided by in-scope accounts. % Not Started represents accounts not yet started divided by in-scope accounts. Final Score is calculated using the weighted logic from the source sheet: 50% Conversion, 30% Coverage, and -20% Not Started. Conversion receives the highest weight because it reflects real execution progress and movement toward value realization. Coverage receives a secondary positive weight because it shows transformation breadth and pipeline activation. Not Started is weighted negatively because it represents activation gap and implementation risk.

Data note: all operational values were recalculated from the latest uploaded workbook and the VALORES sheet.
